title: Buildprint Mapper OS
creator: Agent Buildprint
summary: Agent-session workflow for mapping an existing repo into a source-independent v3 Buildprint packet with a generic AI-builder briefing, product-contract mirror, mandatory UI/style constitution, and comprehensive phase objectives.
plainDescription: Use Mapper OS when you have a working repo and want an agent to turn it into a reusable Buildprint: map the source, classify the artifact, choose scope, then emit a v3 phase-driven execution packet with generic BUILDPRINT.md, 00-questions.md, 01-project-setup.md, detailed 02-uiux-decision.md, blueprint.yaml, 03-phases, and HANDOVER.md.
promise: A repo-to-Buildprint workflow that preserves scope fidelity and emits a readable packet that makes agents build real product loops with design responsibility instead of generic slop.
whatYouGet: A selected-buildprint/ v3 executable packet: generic BUILDPRINT.md, 00-questions.md, 01-project-setup.md, detailed 02-uiux-decision.md, blueprint.yaml, 03-phases/, HANDOVER.md
A BUILDPRINT.md that orients the AI builder with role, responsibility, perfection alignment, fake-success intolerance, and read order only
A blueprint.yaml product-contract mirror for artifact identity, central interface, golden path, runtime/posture constraints, and routing
A mandatory UX/style constitution with UX importance, understandability checklist, design thesis, color tokens, typography, layout, component language, motion, states, and anti-generic rules
Comprehensive phase files that read 02-uiux-decision.md and include How to implement this phase, Building objective, DO NOT, Minimum proof before moving on, and Handoff note
whatYouNeed: GitHub/private repo access if the source is private
A human scope choice when multiple candidates exist
Permission to use any private source code
includes: A selected-buildprint/ v3 executable packet: generic BUILDPRINT.md, 00-questions.md, 01-project-setup.md, detailed 02-uiux-decision.md, blueprint.yaml, 03-phases/, HANDOVER.md
A BUILDPRINT.md that orients the AI builder with role, responsibility, perfection alignment, fake-success intolerance, and read order only
A blueprint.yaml product-contract mirror for artifact identity, central interface, golden path, runtime/posture constraints, and routing
A mandatory UX/style constitution with UX importance, understandability checklist, design thesis, color tokens, typography, layout, component language, motion, states, and anti-generic rules
Comprehensive phase files that read 02-uiux-decision.md and include How to implement this phase, Building objective, DO NOT, Minimum proof before moving on, and Handoff note
risks: Packet overfits source internals instead of observable behavior
Product-specific source baggage leaks into selected BUILDPRINT.md
02-uiux-decision.md becomes a weak moodboard instead of a style constitution
Generated phases become generic dashboard work or skip design responsibility
Agent writes proof-looking prose instead of building a usable loop
Provider/deployment/security boundaries are hidden instead of named
Non-product artifacts are forced into product-app UI phases
checks: Selected output uses v3 phase-driven packet files
BUILDPRINT.md is a generic AI-builder briefing with no mapped-source product specifics
02-uiux-decision.md starts with UX importance/understandability and includes a small checklist
02-uiux-decision.md contains a detailed style constitution, not short phrases
Every phase references 02-uiux-decision.md as standing design/style responsibility
Obsolete v2 packet folders/files are absent from the selected packet
Every phase has required comprehensive headings and a substantial Building objective
Phase flow rejects placeholders, functionless buttons, mocks-as-proof, and fake success
HANDOVER.md names built, verified, blocked, not proven, and next actions

trustBadges.label: Generic builder briefing
trustBadges.detail: BUILDPRINT.md orients the AI builder with role, responsibility, perfection alignment, fake-success intolerance, and read order only.

trustBadges.label: Mandatory UX constitution
trustBadges.detail: 02-uiux-decision.md makes UX understandable, detailed, style-specific, and binding on every UI-bearing phase.

trustBadges.label: Phase-driven execution
trustBadges.detail: Selected packets use comprehensive phase objectives and active-phase flow instead of tiny schema fragments.

trustBadges.label: Anti-fake-success
trustBadges.detail: The packet bans placeholders, dead controls, mocks-as-proof, fake provider success, and overclaiming.

trustBadges.label: Scope-preserving mapper
trustBadges.detail: Mapper OS separates source facts from guesses and asks for a human scope choice when multiple artifacts are plausible.

howToUse.title: 1. Point it at a repo
howToUse.detail: Open the source repo in your coding agent, or give the agent a repo URL/local path. If the target is unclear, Mapper OS asks which repo URL or local path to map.

howToUse.title: 2. Bootstrap Mapper OS
howToUse.detail: Run agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json and follow .buildprint/next-agent.md. Do not use an agb map command.

howToUse.title: 3. Discover, then choose scope
howToUse.detail: The agent inspects the repo and proposes candidates. For broad repos, it stops and asks which candidate to extract.

howToUse.title: 4. Emit the v3 packet
howToUse.detail: The selected packet contains generic BUILDPRINT.md, 00-questions.md, 01-project-setup.md, detailed 02-uiux-decision.md, blueprint.yaml, 03-phases/, and HANDOVER.md.

howToUse.title: 5. Build phase by phase
howToUse.detail: A downstream builder reads the active phase only, keeps 02-uiux-decision.md open as design responsibility, builds the smallest real product path, verifies it, repairs fake-success shortcuts, and records handoff facts.

HANDOVER.md names built, verified, blocked, not proven, and next actions
copyPrompt: Use the Buildprint Mapper OS Buildprint to map a target repo in the current agent session. Bootstrap exact snapshots with agb start https://agent-buildprint.com/buildprints/buildprint-mapper-os/package.json . Then read .buildprint/next-agent.md. Emit a v3 phase-driven packet with generic BUILDPRINT.md, detailed 02-uiux-decision.md, blueprint.yaml product contract, and comprehensive phases.
